highlights.text: Institutions leave their footprint on the chart when they buy a dip and it comes in the form of my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
Institutions long by waiting for a deep elevator down sell, they wait for price to undercut a low, they then trap shorts (usually retail), price recovers the low, and we squeeze the other way.
This is the Failed Breakdown, and my edge is simply following them.
I wrote last Tuesday at 4pm: “The Failed Breakdown of the 11:30AM 6818 low is also actionable.” We got exactly this Wednesday - ES went elevator down from 6865 resistance down to 6817.
The task for bulls today was to keep it going through FOMC but of course, we would need a dip to buy first and institutions buy dips by putting in a Failed Breakdown and trapping shorts.

I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “Instead of buying 6838, one could wait for a Failed Breakdown of Monday’s 6835 daily low.” We swept that down to 6830 this morning, recovered, shorts were trapped and longs triggered.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the quality Failed Breakdown’s we’ve seen the last few days.

post_title: Bulls Bought The FOMC Dip As Expected. Can They Run Now? December 11 Plan
